content.text:
## Task: Fix [medium] UI/UX: Search loading spinner is visual-only; screen readers get no 'loading' announcement
## Step: review-tests

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: medium
File: src/app/render-orchestrator.ts:79-86
Title: Search loading spinner is visual-only; screen readers get no 'loading' announcement

Finding:
When a search is in flight, sighted users see a centered spinning `bi-arrow-repeat`, but the spinner container is a plain `<div>` with no `role='status'` and no `aria-live` region, so assistive-tech users receive no feedback that work is happening (and the previous results simply vanish with no explanation). WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) calls for status messages to be announced programmatically. Mark the spinner wrapper as a polite live region so its presence is announced when it appears.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/render-orchestrator.ts, where the spinner is constructed (the `if (route.view === 'search')` block that builds `spinnerWrap` and `spinnerIcon`), add `spinnerWrap.setAttribute('role', 'status');` and `spinnerWrap.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Loading search results');` after creating `spinnerWrap`. Keep the spinner icon itself hidden from AT per the decorative-icons finding (it already has no text content). Do not change the visual styling.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The existing test suite for the search loading spinner covers the visual presence and lifecycle of the spinner but does not verify the accessibility attributes required by the fix (role='status' and aria-label='Loading search results' on the spinner wrapper). The current tests are well-structured and follow project patterns, but they lack coverage for the new WCAG 4.1.3 status message requirements. The fix instructions specify adding these attributes to the spinner wrapper, yet no test asserts their presence. Additionally, the existing test for aria-hidden='true' on the icon (lines 610-630) is a separate accessibility concern and does not cover the requested live region.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }

When a search is in flight, sighted users see a centered spinning `bi-arrow-repeat`, but the spinner container is a plain `<div>` with no `role='status'` and no `aria-live` region, so assistive-tech users receive no feedback that work is happening (and the previous results simply vanish with no explanation). WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) calls for status messages to be announced programmatically. Mark the spinner wrapper as a polite live region so its presence is announced when it appears.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/render-orchestrator.ts, where the spinner is constructed (the `if (route.view === 'search')` block that builds `spinnerWrap` and `spinnerIcon`), add `spinnerWrap.setAttribute('role', 'status');` and `spinnerWrap.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Loading search results');` after creating `spinnerWrap`.
